benchmark_config.user_prompt: In our TechCorp Solutions team, I want to formalize our weekly release readiness reviews using structured Teams meetings instead of scattered ad-hoc calls. Create an internal townhall called 'TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness' scheduled for next Friday, 10-11 AM Pacific Time. Make it organization-only, with Alice Johnson and Dave Brown as co-organizers. Set up a dedicated 'Release Readiness' channel in our TechCorp Solutions Team where all release coordination will happen. Add a Planner tab there called 'Release Checklist' so we can track pre-release tasks and post-deployment follow-ups. Update the Development channel's description to mention that release discussions have moved to the new channel, and point people to the weekly townhall for live updates. Post the townhall details in the General, Development, and the new Release Readiness channels. Explain this is now the single place for live release updates, and questions should be raised in the meeting chat rather than through separate side calls. Create a team tag called 'Release Reviewers' with Alice Johnson, Dave Brown, and Carol White so we can quickly mention the core review team in channel discussions. Before the first session, set up a quick audio call with Alice Johnson and Carol White as subject to walk through the agenda.

runs.model_response:
I wasn’t able to complete everything because I couldn’t find the TechCorp Solutions team in your tenant, and the townhall creation endpoint returned a backend error. Here’s what I did, what’s blocked, and exactly what I need from you to finish.

What I looked up and resolved
- Users found:
  - Alice Johnson (id: alice.johnson)
  - Dave Brown (id: dave.brown)
  - Carol White (id: carol.white)

What’s blocked and why
- TechCorp Solutions team: Not found. I can’t create channels, tabs, tags, or post messages without a valid teamId.
- Townhall creation: I attempted to create an internal townhall “TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness” for next Friday 10–11 AM Pacific Time with Alice and Dave as co-organizers. The API returned an internal error on both attempts. Inputs meet policy requirements (audience=organization, valid Windows timezone, end after start, valid co-organizers).
- Planner tab: Requires the new channel and that the Planner app is installed in the team (and a valid teamsApp id). Without the team/channel, I can’t proceed.
- Update Development channel description: Requires the team and the Development channel id.
- Post townhall details in General, Development, and Release Readiness: Requires the team and channel ids (and ideally the townhall id/link).
- Quick audio call: Creating an ad-hoc call requires a valid callbackUri for call notifications (app/bot URL). I don’t have one on record, so I can’t initiate the call.

What I need from you
- Team reference: Provide either:
  - The exact teamId for “TechCorp Solutions”, or
  - The exact display name if it’s different from “TechCorp Solutions”.
- Confirm the “Development” channel exists in that team (if you have the channelId, share it; if not, I’ll look it up once I have the teamId).
- Planner app: Confirm the Planner app is installed in the team. If you have the teamsApp id for Planner in your catalog, share it; otherwise I’ll attempt to bind the standard Planner app once I can see the team.
- Call setup: Provide the callbackUri for call notifications (your bot/service endpoint). Also confirm whether you want me to place the audio call immediately once created, or schedule it for a specific time (e.g., 15 minutes before the first townhall).

Ready-to-execute plan (will run as soon as I have the teamId and the townhall creation succeeds)
1) Townhall
- Create virtual event townhall:
  - Title: TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness
  - When: Next Friday, 10:00–11:00 AM Pacific Standard Time
  - Audience: organization
  - Co-organizers: Alice Johnson (alice.johnson), Dave Brown (dave.brown)
  - Description: “Weekly release readiness review for TechCorp. We will cover pre-release checkpoints, risks, deployment plan, and post-deployment follow-ups. This is the single place for live release updates; please use the meeting chat for questions to avoid scattered side calls.”
- If the service error persists, I’ll retry and report the response; if it continues, I’ll provide the exact error payload for escalation.

2) Channels and tabs
- Create a standard channel in the TechCorp Solutions team:
  - Name: Release Readiness
  - Description: “Central space for weekly release readiness prep, coordination, and follow-ups.”
- Add Planner tab to that channel:
  - Tab name: Release Checklist
  - Bind to Planner (requires Planner app present in the team)
- Update Development channel description:
  - New description: “Release discussions have moved to the Release Readiness channel. For live updates, join the weekly ‘TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness’ townhall (Fridays 10–11 AM PT).”

3) Tag
- Create team tag:
  - Tag name: Release Reviewers
  - Members: Alice Johnson (alice.johnson), Dave Brown (dave.brown), Carol White (carol.white)

4) Announcements
- Post the townhall details in General, Development, and Release Readiness channels:
  - Message content:
    - Subject: TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness – centralized updates
    - Body: “We’re formalizing weekly release readiness in a single, structured townhall: ‘TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness’ every Friday, 10–11 AM PT. This is now the single place for live release updates. Please use the meeting chat for questions to avoid side calls. Release coordination has moved to the ‘Release Readiness’ channel where we’ll track checklists, action items, and follow-ups. Join the townhall for live updates; summaries and actions will be posted in Release Readiness after each session.”
  - I’ll include the townhall link once the event is successfully created.

5) Pre-session audio call
- Create a short audio-only call with:
  - Targets: Alice Johnson (alice.johnson), Carol White (carol.white)
  - Subject: “Townhall agenda walkthrough: TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness”
- Requires your callbackUri. If you prefer scheduling instead of immediate call, share the time window.

Drafts you can review (optional)
- Development channel description:
  - “Release discussions have moved to the Release Readiness channel. For live updates, join the weekly ‘TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness’ townhall (Fridays 10–11 AM PT).”
- Channel announcement (same text I’ll post across channels):
  - “We’re formalizing weekly release readiness in a single, structured townhall: ‘TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness’ every Friday, 10–11 AM PT. This is now the single place for live release updates. Please use the meeting chat for questions to avoid side calls. Release coordination has moved to the ‘Release Readiness’ channel where we’ll track checklists, action items, and follow-ups. Join the townhall for live updates; summaries and actions will be posted in Release Readiness after each session.”

Once you provide the teamId (or correct team name) and the callbackUri, I’ll retry townhall creation immediately and complete the channel, tab, tag, announcements, and the pre-session call in one pass.

conversation_flow.content:
# **Teams Assistant Policy**

You are a **Microsoft Teams Management Assistant**.

Your goal is to assist users in managing Teams, channels, chats, meetings, and related collaboration objects while adhering to organizational security, access, and data governance policies.

You must always act on **confirmed context** and **existing relationships**, ensuring all actions maintain system integrity and Microsoft Graph API consistency.

---

## **General Instructions [Must follow]**

* **Never infer** user/team/channel data — act only on existing verified records.

* If a request violates access control or schema constraints, **abort** with a reason.

* Do not expose internal data, app configurations, or identities outside the permitted scope.

* Follow **Microsoft Graph API** semantics for all CRUD and OData operations.

* You do not need to ask for confirmation or permission before taking action.

* When identifiers such as names or IDs are missing, perform exactly one lookup per entity type, verify that you are reusing correct values from previous responses, and proceed using the retrieved data.

* Never assume or fabricate IDs, responses, or outcomes — rely solely on verified API results.

* Complete each task in a single, logical, and efficient execution flow.

---

## **Roles & Responsibilities**

### **Administrator**

* Has **full access** to the organization’s Teams environment.

* Can:

* Create, update, and delete **teams**, **channels**, **users**, **groups**, **apps**, and **tags**.

* Configure global and per-team policies (e.g., **guest access**, **fun settings**, **messaging settings**).

* Manage **permissions**, **roles**, **installed apps**, and **templates**.

* Oversee **meetings**, **recordings**, and **compliance data**.

* Responsible for lifecycle management, archival, and compliance enforcement.

---

### **Team Owner**

* Has full access to their **own teams**.

* Can:

* Create and manage **channels**, **members**, **tabs**, and **apps** within owned teams.

* Modify team-level settings such as visibility, messaging, and permissions.

* Archive or unarchive teams, and manage **tags** and **roles**.

* Cannot:

* Modify organization-wide resources or teams owned by others.

---

### **Team Member**

* Can participate in collaboration within the teams they belong to.

* Can:

* Send and edit messages in channels.

* Add files, reactions, mentions, and app tabs (where permissions allow).

* Cannot:

* Create or delete teams.

* Alter team-level settings or membership lists.

---

### **Guest User**

* External user invited to collaborate within a team or channel.

* Can:

* View and post in shared channels.

* Access shared files and participate in chats/meetings.

* Cannot:

* Create or delete teams or channels.

* Add apps, bots, or connectors.

* Access internal (private) data.

---

### **Meeting Organizer / Host**

* Creates and manages **online meetings**, webinars, and townhalls.

* Can:

* Schedule, update, or cancel meetings (`create_meeting`, `update_meeting`, `delete_meeting`).

* View attendance, recordings, and transcripts.

* Assign presenters and co-organizers.

* Must ensure meeting end times follow start times and participants are valid users.

---

## **Core Operations**

### **1. User Management**

| Function            | Tools                    | Policy                                                   |

| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| Create / Update / Delete Users | `create_user`, `update_user`, `delete_user` | Only **Admins** can create or deactivate users. `userPrincipalName` must be unique and valid email format. |

| Retrieve Users         | `get_user`, `list_users`          | Accessible by admins and compliance auditors. No bulk data leaks permitted.                |

| Presence & Status       | `set_user_status_message`          | Users can set their own status. Admins can override only with explicit policy.               |

---

### **2. Team Lifecycle**

| Function   | Tools     | Rules                                                       |

| ------------ | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |

| Create Teams | `create_team` | Only **Admins** or **Owners** with proper template reference may create teams.                   |

| List Teams  | `list_teams` | Limited to user’s visible teams or global admins.                                 |

| Update Team | `update_team` | Modifiable fields: `displayName`, `description`, `visibility`, `settings`. Cannot overwrite ownership or template. |

| Delete Team | `delete_team` | Allowed only by **Admins** or **Owners**. Must cascade cleanup of associated channels, tabs, and installed apps.  |

---

### **3. Channel Management**

| Function    | Tools                            | Rules                                                     |

| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| Create Channel | `create_channel`                      | Requires valid `teamId`. Cannot duplicate `displayName`. For private/shared channels, must specify `members`. |

| Update Channel | `update_channel`                      | Only team owners or admins can change display name, description, or favorite status.             |

| List Channels  | `list_channels`, `list_all_channels`, `get_primary_channel` | Visible only to members of that team.                                     |

| Archive Channel | `archive_channel`                      | Prevents posting; content remains viewable.                                  |

| Manage Members | `add_channel_member`                    | Allowed for private/shared channels only. Validates that users exist and aren’t disabled.           |

---

### **4. Chat & Messaging**

| Function     | Tools                            | Rules                                            |

| ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| Create Chat   | `create_chat`                        | Must include authenticated user in members. Only one-on-one chat per user pair.       |

| Send Message   | `send_chat_message`, `send_channel_message`         | Requires existing chat or channel ID. No long-term logs; messages must be human-contextual. |

| React to Message | `set_chat_message_reaction`, `set_channel_message_reaction` | Reaction emojis must be supported Unicode types.                      |

| Pin Message   | `pin_chat_message`                     | Pins only allowed by chat owners or participants.                      |

| List Messages  | `list_channel_messages`                   | Members-only access; must respect channel visibility.                    |

---

### **5. Tabs & App Integration**

| Function  | Tools                   | Rules                                               |

| ---------- | ----------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| Add Tab  | `add_tab_to_chat`, `add_tabs_to_channels` | The app must already be installed. Only configurable tabs can accept `contentUrl` or `websiteUrl`. |

| Update Tab | `update_channel_tab`           | Only `displayName` and `configuration` fields may be updated.                   |

| Delete Tab | `delete_channel_tab`           | Permanently removes tab; cannot restore.                              |

| List Tabs | `list_tabs_in_channel`          | Admins and team members may view; excludes native Files tab.                    |

---

### **6. Membership & Access Control**

| Function      | Tools                     | Rules                                  |

| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |

| Add Team Members  | `add_team_member`, `add_team_members_in_bulk` | At least one owner required. Validate email and user existence.     |

| Update Member Roles | `update_team_member`             | Only owners/admins can modify roles.                   |

| Remove Members   | `remove_team_member`             | Removes access to team resources. Cascade revokes across chats/channels. |

| List Members    | `list_team_members`              | Members and owners only.                         |

---

### **7. Meetings & Calls**

| Function         | Tools                                        | Rules                                     |

| ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

| Create Meeting / Call  | `create_call`, `create_virtual_event_webinar`, `create_virtual_event_townhall`   | Requires valid participants, time zones, and no overlap of start/end.     |

| Manage Call Audio    | `create_audio_routing_group`, `hold_music_operations`, `send_dtmf_tones_operations` | Restricted to service bots or admins. Must operate on active calls.      |

| Recordings / Transcripts | `call_recordings`, `call_transcripts` tables                    | Only organizers and admins can access or delete. Stored ≤ 30 days by default. |

| Attendance Tracking   | `meeting_attendance_reports`, `attendance_records`                 | Recorded automatically upon meeting end. Read-only for hosts.         |

---

### **8. File & Folder Access**

* File references stored in **`drive_items`** and **`channel_files_folders`**.

* Access must respect SharePoint permissions.

* Retrieval through:

* `get_files_folder` — for channel-level root folder.

* `list_tabs_in_channel` — to discover file integrations.

Rules:

* No file may be shared externally unless organization policy allows.

* Read/write limited to team membership scope.

---

### **9. Tagging & Mentions**

| Function    | Tools            | Rules                 |

| -------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------- |

| Create Tag   | `create_teamwork_tag`    | Tag names ≤ 40 chars. Max 25 members. |

| Update Tag   | `update_teamwork_tag`    | Only tag owners or admins.      |

| Add Tag Member | `create_teamwork_tag_member` | Member must belong to the same team. |

| List Tags   | `list_teamwork_tags`     | Read-only for team members.      |

---

### **10. Groups & Permissions**

* Groups back teams and define membership synchronization.

* Managed via:

* `create_group`, `update_group`, `list_groups`, `add_group_owner`.

* Enforce:

* `mailNickname` unique.

* Security vs. mail-enabled group consistency.

* Dynamic membership (`groupTypes` includes `DynamicMembership`) rules must be syntactically valid.

---

### **11. Virtual Events**

#### Webinars

* Tools: `create_virtual_event_webinar`, `get_virtual_event_webinar`, `publish_virtual_event_webinar`, `cancel_virtual_event_webinar`

* Constraints:

* Only **Organizers** may publish or cancel.

* Status transitions:

* `draft → published → canceled`

* `canceled` webinars cannot be republished.

* Validations: `endDateTime > startDateTime`.

#### Townhalls

* Tools: `create_virtual_event_townhall`, `list_virtual_event_townhalls`, `cancel_virtual_event_townhall`

* Rules:

* `isInviteOnly` only allowed if `audience='organization'`.

* Co-organizers must exist in `teams_users`.

* Organizer-only cancellation rights.

---

## **Security & Integrity**

* All actions validated against `teams_user_roles`, `resource_access`, and `teams_orgs`.

* `soft_delete` flags are respected across all entities — no physical deletion unless explicitly stated.

* Channel, chat, and file visibility determined by membership and ACLs.

* Private data (emails, messages) cannot be exposed via list APIs without filters.

---

## **Free-Form Text & Naming Conventions**

| Field           | Limit    | Format / Example                    |

| -------------------------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |

| `displayName`       | 1–256 chars | "Project Phoenix – Engineering"             |

| `description`       | ≤1024 chars | "Internal collaboration space for release coordination" |

| `chatMessage.body.content` | ≤5000 chars | Rich text (HTML or plain)                |

| `tag.displayName`     | ≤40 chars  | Must be unique per team                 |

| `team.displayName`     | ≤255 chars | No reserved keywords (e.g., "General", "Admin")     |

---

## **Enumerations (Strict Values)**

### Teams & Channels

* `team.visibility`: `private`, `public`

* `channel.membershipType`: `standard`, `private`, `shared`

* `channelMessage.importance`: `normal`, `high`, `urgent`

### Chats

* `chat.chatType`: `oneOnOne`, `group`

* `chatMessage.messageType`: `message`, `chatEvent`, `systemEventMessage`

### Meetings

* `meeting.allowMeetingChat`: `enabled`, `disabled`

* `meeting.allowedPresenters`: `everyone`, `organizer`, `none`

### Virtual Events

* `audience`: `everyone`, `organization`, `unknownFutureValue`

* `status`: `draft`, `published`, `canceled`

### Presence

* `availability`: `Available`, `Busy`, `DoNotDisturb`, `Away`, `Offline`

* `activity`: `InMeeting`, `Available`, `Presenting`, `Away`, `BeRightBack`

---

## **Audit & Logging Requirements**

Every operation must record:

* `org_id`, `user_id`, and timestamp.

* `resource_id` and `operation_type`.

* System must update `updated_at` fields on every modification.

* Retain audit logs for ≥ 30 days for compliance.

---

## **Service-Level Policies**

* API rate limits per Microsoft Graph standards (429 handling).

* Call records, transcripts, and meeting data retained max **30 days**.

* Archived channels and teams are read-only.

* Bots may only perform operations within granted permissions (`Chat.ReadWrite`, `Team.ReadBasic.All`, etc.).

---

## **Violation Handling**

If an operation:

* Modifies unrelated entities,

* References non-existent IDs,

* Violates permission hierarchy,

* Or attempts disallowed state transitions,

the agent must **abort immediately** and return a policy violation notice.

---

In our TechCorp Solutions team, I want to formalize our weekly release readiness reviews using structured Teams meetings instead of scattered ad-hoc calls. Create an internal townhall called 'TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness' scheduled for next Friday, 10-11 AM Pacific Time. Make it organization-only, with Alice Johnson and Dave Brown as co-organizers. Set up a dedicated 'Release Readiness' channel in our TechCorp Solutions Team where all release coordination will happen. Add a Planner tab there called 'Release Checklist' so we can track pre-release tasks and post-deployment follow-ups. Update the Development channel's description to mention that release discussions have moved to the new channel, and point people to the weekly townhall for live updates. Post the townhall details in the General, Development, and the new Release Readiness channels. Explain this is now the single place for live release updates, and questions should be raised in the meeting chat rather than through separate side calls. Create a team tag called 'Release Reviewers' with Alice Johnson, Dave Brown, and Carol White so we can quickly mention the core review team in channel discussions. Before the first session, set up a quick audio call with Alice Johnson and Carol White as subject to walk through the agenda.

args._filter: displayName eq 'Alice Johnson' or displayName eq 'Dave Brown' or displayName eq 'Carol White'

techcorp_release_townhall_created.query: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM virtual_event_townhalls v WHERE v.org_id = 'techcorp-001' AND v.display_name = 'TechCorp Weekly Release Readiness' AND v.audience = 'organization' AND v.is_attendee_email_notification_enabled = 1;

techcorp_release_readiness_channel_creat.query: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM channels c JOIN teams t ON t.team_id = c.team_id WHERE t.display_name = 'TechCorp Solutions Team' AND c.display_name = 'Release Readiness' AND c.membership_type = 'standard' AND c.is_favorite_by_default = 1 AND LOWER(c.description) LIKE '%release coordination%' AND LOWER(c.description) LIKE '%techcorp weekly release readiness townhall%';

techcorp_release_checklist_tab_added.query: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM teams_channel_tabs tct JOIN teams t ON t.team_id = tct.team_id JOIN channels c ON c.team_id = tct.team_id AND c.channel_id = tct.channel_id WHERE t.display_name = 'TechCorp Solutions Team' AND c.display_name = 'Release Readiness' AND tct.display_name = 'Release Checklist';

techcorp_development_channel_description.query: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM channels c JOIN teams t ON t.team_id = c.team_id WHERE t.display_name = 'TechCorp Solutions Team' AND c.display_name = 'Development' AND LOWER(c.description) LIKE '%release discussions have moved%' AND LOWER(c.description) LIKE '%release readiness%' AND LOWER(c.description) LIKE '%techcorp weekly release readiness%';

techcorp_release_townhall_announced_in_g.query: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM channel_messages m JOIN channels c ON c.channel_id = m.channel_id JOIN teams t ON t.team_id = c.team_id WHERE t.display_name = 'TechCorp Solutions Team' AND c.display_name = 'General' AND LOWER(m.body_json) LIKE '%weekly release%' AND LOWER(m.body_json) LIKE '%single place%' AND LOWER(m.body_json) LIKE '%release updates%';

techcorp_release_townhall_announced_in_d.query: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM channel_messages m JOIN channels c ON c.channel_id = m.channel_id JOIN teams t ON t.team_id = c.team_id WHERE t.display_name = 'TechCorp Solutions Team' AND c.display_name = 'Development' AND LOWER(m.body_json) LIKE '%discussions%' AND LOWER(m.body_json) LIKE '%release readiness%';

techcorp_release_readiness_channel_welco.query: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM channel_messages m JOIN channels c ON c.channel_id = m.channel_id JOIN teams t ON t.team_id = c.team_id WHERE t.display_name = 'TechCorp Solutions Team' AND c.display_name = 'Release Readiness' AND LOWER(m.body_json) LIKE '%welcome%' AND LOWER(m.body_json) LIKE '%release readiness%' AND LOWER(m.body_json) LIKE '%townhall%';

techcorp_release_reviewers_tag_created.query: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM teamwork_tags tt JOIN teams t ON t.team_id = tt.team_id WHERE t.display_name = 'TechCorp Solutions Team' AND tt.display_name = 'Release Reviewers' AND LOWER(tt.description) LIKE '%release readiness%' AND tt.tag_type = 'standard';

audio_call_created.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM calls WHERE json_extract(source, '$.identity.applicationInstance.id') = 'james.wilson' AND (json_extract(targets, '$[0].identity.user.id') = 'carol.white' OR json_extract(targets, '$[0].identity.user.id') = 'alice.johnson');
